records.ety: [F. complexion, fr. L. complexio. See Complex, a.]

defs: The state of being complex; complexity.
quotes: Though the terms of propositions may be complex, yet . . . it is properly called a simple syllogism, since the complexion does not belong to the syllogistic form of it.
This paragraph is . . . a complexion of sophisms.

defs: The bodily constitution; the temperament; habitude, or natural disposition; character; nature.
quotes: If his complexion incline him to melancholy.
It is the complexion of them all to leave the dam.

defs: The color or hue of the skin, esp. of the face.
quotes: Tall was her stature, her complexion dark.
Between the pale complexion of true love, And the red glow of scorn and proud disdain.

defs: The general appearance or aspect; as, the complexion of the sky; the complexion of the news.
